evidence_sentence: Chr09:62,620,720 minor allele (near mir172a): Earlier flowering (p=5.93×10⁻¹⁷ Nebraska, p=7.80×10⁻⁵ Alabama).
SbSBP19/SbSBP4 high expression: Earlier flowering (FDR = 4.37×10⁻⁹, validated Alabama p=5.38×10⁻⁵).
sbi-MIR156h high expression: Represses SBP factors → delayed flowering.
SbFT8 minor allele (G at Chr03:69,067,236): Earlier flowering (~3-5 day difference, p=2.53×10⁻⁹ Nebraska).
Day-neutral allele: early, consistent flowering regardless of daylength; reduction-of-function from 3-bp CAAT-box deletion.
two conferred statistically significant delays averaging 13.1 (P = 0.03) and 24.8 days (P = 0.09) when carrying short-day S. propinquum alleles.
44M ma2 ma3_R: Intermediate LD flowering; flowers earlier and has more transcripts than 100M.
notes: 44M = ma2 ma3^R double recessive (not PRR37 as gene field suggests). Intermediate LD earliness.

evidence_sentence: 100M (Ma1 Ma2 Ma3 Ma6 dominant): Very Late LD flowering but rapidly in short days.
notes: 100M (dominant Ma1 Ma2 Ma3 Ma6) WT under LD — canonical late-flowering reference.

evidence_sentence: 100M...flowers very late in LDs but rapidly in short days (SDs).
notes: Photoperiod-sensitive WT under SD; SD flowers rapid vs LD-default baseline.

evidence_sentence: Theis (sweet sorghum, sensitive) Ma1: Very Late LD flowering; Leaf number: LD ~19 vs SD ≤9.
notes: Theis (photoperiod-sensitive Ma1 cultivar) under LD. Treated as WT+LD; cultivar-level genetics are WT-like.

evidence_sentence: SbPRR37 low evening expression (LD) associated with early flowering.
Ghd7 suppression: Earlier flowering via loss of LD repression (breeding target).
In a dominant Ma4 background, a dominant allele at Ma2 delays flowering, while in a recessive ma4 background, Ma2 has a minimal impact.
notes: Ma4 locus not molecularly cloned; no corresponding network node.

evidence_sentence: qFT6b → Likely corresponds to Ma1 (PRR37); effect size 5.3-7.1 days in kafir families.
SbEhd1 is a key flowering activator in sorghum, directly regulating florigen genes.
Purifying selection has acted on genes involved in photoperiodic control (CCA1, LHY, TOC1, GI).
GIGANTEA SNP888: +8 day effect on PRI (increases photoperiod sensitivity).
CRY1-b1 SNP722 (T→A transition): -4.2 day effect on PRI (reduces photoperiod sensitivity).
notes: SbCRY1 (cryptochrome) not modelled in network; Blue_Light source represents blue-light input but does not map to CRY1 directly.

evidence_sentence: Sorghum is a short-day plant; night-break during SD reverses the induction to LD-like flowering delay.
notes: Night-break during SD mimics LD photoperiodic signal; encoded as LD-like Photoperiod_LD boost.

evidence_sentence: The dw2 mutation is tightly linked to a maturation gene, ma1; a strong QTL of flowering time overlaps this region.
notes: dw2 is a GA-biosynthesis dwarfing gene linked to the Ma1 region but not a flowering-time node in this network.

evidence_sentence: Dim, far-red-enriched light duplicates the mutant's shade-avoidance phenotype in wild-type plants.
notes: Dim far-red supplementation converts phyB to inactive Pr form; modeled as PHYB knockdown (gm=0.5).

evidence_sentence: CO has greater activity in SD compared to LD causing up to 10-fold higher expression of SbCN8/12 during the night.
SbFT10...major florigen.
Three genes, designated as SbFT1, SbFT8 and SbFT10, behave as functional Hd3a/RFT1/ZCN8 orthologs.
notes: Triple florigen knockout (SbFT1+SbFT8+SbFT10) — all three mapped to individual network paralogs.

ortholog of the maize gene rap2.7 which acts as a negative regulator of flowering time.

notes: SbFT2 paralog not modelled; network contains FT1, FT8, FT10, FT12 florigen paralogs only.
